assessor_raw: The churn model achieves strong retrospective prediction performance, but the proposal leaps from this to recommending automatic discount deployment. This leap requires an intervening empirical claim — that discounts cause the identified high-risk subscribers to stay — which is not supported by the presented evidence. The historical observation that agent-assigned discount recipients had lower observed churn is not causal evidence applicable to model-directed offers. The agent selection mechanism (complaints, competitive threats) and the model selection mechanism (behavioral features) select for different customer types with potentially different responsiveness to discount offers. A subscriber who is leaving because of a service quality issue will not be retained by a discount. The proposal acknowledges these gaps (no randomized experiment, no margin analysis, no price sensitivity analysis) but draws a launch recommendation from them anyway. The correct action is a randomized pilot.
